title: Robometer: Scaling General-Purpose Robotic Reward Models via Trajectory Comparisons
summary: Robometer is a dense, general-purpose video-language reward model trained via dual objectives: frame-level progress and success prediction on expert demonstrations, and trajectory-comparison preference learning from both optimal and suboptimal data. The work introduces RBM-1M, a large-scale reward-learning dataset comprising over one million trajectories spanning diverse robot embodiments and tasks. Robometer is evaluated across six downstream use cases including online RL, offline RL, model-based planning, failure detection, and data retrieval, demonstrating broad applicability as a general robotic reward signal.
